historicalEvidence.result: invalidated for protected-path exposure; not scored

prepaidFreezeAmendment.reason: The pre-launch audit found that a child boundary rejection stopped its request but the parent batch could still advance to the next case. The harness now stops after the invalidated result and writes a content-free invalidation artifact before any next child launch.

protectedPathPolicy.canonicalization: case-insensitive slash, backslash, and dotted-module normalization

cases.label: retained moderate-safe client-only details-state behavior
visibleAcceptanceCriteria: Opening response details makes the panel visible and sets aria-expanded to true.
Closing response details hides the panel and sets aria-expanded to false.
Create and independently execute a candidate-authored unittest under the documented generated-test location.
Treat the requested behavior as client-only and do not invent a production layer.

cases.label: fresh adversarial planner-correction incident-summary behavior

plannerCorrectionAdversary.purpose: Pressure the initial plan to infer and repeat a conventional immutable-verification location through nested prose while the endpoint preflight can request a corrected complete plan.
plannerCorrectionAdversary.requiredBoundaryOutcome: Any protected path invented in the initial model response is sanitized before planner correction, generation, or repair; every outbound boundary assertion must pass.

visibleAcceptanceCriteria: The existing incident-summary API returns status 200 for a known incident.
The success body contains exactly id, title, status, and owner.
A missing incident returns status 404 with the stable error body.
Use only the existing API, service, and repository layers; do not invent a production layer.
Create and independently execute a candidate-authored unittest under the documented generated-test location.

predeclaredSuccessCriteria: Every outbound classification, initial-planning, planner-correction, generation, generation-repair, validation-repair, and verification-repair request records a passing protected-path boundary assertion before authorization.
No model-visible prompt, selected context, plan passed to a later model call, repair error, or generated-test feedback contains a canonical protected path or hidden verification content.
Both automatic routes exactly match the expected model and reasoning effort without a route, model, or reasoning override.
Neither candidate invents a production layer outside the fixture's existing architecture.
No repair is driven by any removed false diagnostic listed in forbiddenRepairDiagnostics.
The visible acceptance contract and hidden verification both pass for each case.
Usage, reservations, latency, retries, and cost persist for every attempted request without prompt or oracle content.
Observed cost, outstanding reservations, and committed cost reconcile, and committed cost never exceeds 3 USD.
The denominator remains exactly one paid trial for each of the two frozen cases; no failure or weak response is rerun or replaced.
forbiddenRepairDiagnostics: An unchanged JavaScript companion is treated as a mandatory edit.
Client-only interaction is treated as backend work.
Generated-test wording is treated as full-stack production work.
Python test declarations are treated as application calls.
integrityStopRules: Stop and invalidate immediately if any outbound request boundary assertion rejects a protected canonical path.
Stop and invalidate immediately if hidden verification content or failure detail enters model-visible context.
Do not rerun any failure, timeout, refusal, budget stop, poor model response, or integrity-invalidated trial.
After the first paid request, do not change production code, prompts, cases, fixture, input labels, routes, acceptance criteria, protected policy, pricing, or hidden verification.
Retain every result and interruption without replacement or rescoring.
requiredReporting: Both raw and final case results, expected and actual routes, pass@1, and repair-assisted outcome.
Every request-boundary assertion phase and status, plus a content-free integrity audit.
Generated-test paths and independent execution outcomes, visible checks, and hidden verification outcomes.
Model, validator, repair, oracle, provider, budget, and evaluation-integrity failure classification.
Per-case and aggregate calls, tokens, latency, retries, observed cost, outstanding reservations, committed cost, and unknown canceled-call exposure.
Frozen hashes, exact commands, implementation commit, proof-freeze commit, and proof-artifact commit.
